macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US PCE Inflation Jumps 0.7% in One Month, Largest Gain Since June 2022
items.summary: PCE inflation, the Federal Reserve’s preferred indicator, surged by 0.7% in a single month amid stalled Middle East war negotiations impacting economies globally. Markets now price in no Fed rate cuts for all of 2026, with rate hikes expected for BoE, BoJ, and ECB.

items.title: Apple's Q1 Earnings Beat Wall Street Expectations
items.summary: Apple reported first-quarter profits that exceeded analyst forecasts, driven by growth in investment values and improved business performance. Details provided by CBS News contributor on the earnings beat.

items.title: US Gas Prices Surge 35 Cents in Past Week Due to Strait of Hormuz Blockade
items.summary: President Trump announced U.S. guidance for trapped ships through the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran war, as gas prices rise sharply. Missouri gas prices now exceed $4.44 per gallon due to supply disruptions.

items.title: Government Bond Yields Rise Across Advanced Economies, US 10-Year Up 7 Basis Points
items.summary: Bond yields increased globally led by a 7-basis-point rise in the US 10-year Treasury, reflecting higher inflation expectations. The dollar weakened slightly due to Yen appreciation after Japanese authorities' warning.

items.title: Upcoming US Jobs Report Expected to Show 62K Job Gains, Moderation from March
items.summary: Consensus forecasts predict 62,000 monthly job creations versus March's 178,000, with unemployment steady at 4.3%. Key data releases this week include UK/Eurozone PMIs and China's trade data.

items.title: Earnings Watch: Airbnb, AMD, Disney, McDonald’s, Uber Set to Report
items.summary: Investors anticipate broad market signals from key companies including Airbnb, AMD, Arm, BMW, Disney, HSBC, McDonald’s, Palantir, Shell, and Uber this week.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $80K as Bulls Regain Control
items.summary: Bitcoin has pumped over $80,000, faking out bears, with analysts predicting potential rise to $85K this week amid bullish momentum. BlackRock's European Bitcoin ETP surpasses $1.1B in assets, holding ~14,200 BTC.[1]

items.title: NYSE Files Rule for Tokenized Equities and ETFs Trading
items.summary: The New York Stock Exchange proposed a rule change with the SEC to enable tokenized versions of eligible equities and ETFs to trade under DTC's three-year tokenization pilot program, sharing same CUSIP and rights.[1]

items.title: US Military Researches Bitcoin for National Defense
items.summary: A four-star admiral testified to Congress that the US military is actively researching Bitcoin as a weapon for national defense, including running a Bitcoin node to protect against Chinese cyberattacks amid concerns over China-made mining hardware.[4]

items.title: Solana Attracts Major Inflows as Institutional Magnet
items.summary: Solana stablecoin daily active users hit a new all-time high, up 236% in four months, with $381 million in ecosystem inflows over the last three months, 69% from Ethereum, positioning it as a key institutional hub second to Ethereum.[6]

items.title: BlackRock Urges OCC to Expand Tokenized Assets in GENIUS Act
items.summary: BlackRock submitted a comment letter to the OCC urging the removal of tokenized reserve caps and expansion of eligible assets under the GENIUS Act.[1]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Study warns cost-cutting use of generative AI could increase cyber-attack risks
items.summary: A new study highlights that while generative AI can reduce costs in machine-learning systems, it increases cybersecurity risks including cyber-attacks, data breaches, and bias against underrepresented groups.

items.title: Anthropic sued by Defense Department over supply chain designation
items.summary: The U.S. military formally designated AI firm Anthropic a supply chain risk, prompting the company to sue the Defense Department and other federal agencies. The military has ordered the removal of Anthropic's products from systems within 180 days.

items.title: Massachusetts doctors increasingly adopting AI diagnostic tools
items.summary: Medical professionals are integrating AI platforms like OpenEvidence and AI-powered imaging tools into clinical practice, with some AI systems reducing radiation exposure by 30-50% while maintaining image quality.

items.title: Spotify launches badge to identify human artists amid AI music surge
items.summary: As AI-generated music proliferates on the platform, Spotify introduced a new badge system to help users identify and trust the authenticity of music created by human artists.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Clash of Clans Launches Clash vs Skeleton Event with Free Hero Skin
items.summary: The new Clash vs Skeleton event is live now, offering a free legendary hero skin, scenery, and decorations, running until May 18th.

items.title: The Division 2: New Prototype Gear Farm Discovered in Open World
items.summary: A new method for farming prototype gear has been found in the open world of The Division 2, available for several days but costing tokens.

items.title: ropz Shares Shocking Reveal After Vitality's CS2 Victory
items.summary: In an exclusive interview, Counter-Strike player ropz discusses a surprising reveal following Vitality's recent win.

items.title: Marathon Called 'Greatest Flop' in New Reaction Video
items.summary: Aztecross reacts to a video praising Bungie's Marathon as the greatest 'flop' ever played, highlighting its unique situation.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Amazon opens logistics networks to third-party businesses
items.summary: Amazon has opened its logistics networks to external businesses, with major companies like P&G, 3M, Lands' End, and American Eagle among the first to sign up.

items.title: Nissan abandons US EV plant plans
items.summary: Nissan has abandoned its plans for a US electric vehicle manufacturing plant, ending expectations for 200,000 EVs annually from the facility.

items.title: Microsoft's Xbox mode rolling out to Windows 11 PCs
items.summary: Microsoft is bringing Xbox mode to Windows 11, introducing a console-like interface to personal computers.

items.title: Ask.com shuts down
items.summary: Ask.com, the original AI search bot, has officially shut down, marking the end of an era for the internet search service.
